zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Bokill於1400年出生在英國素福爾可可諾迪沙拉爾 煙雨 布可洛福時，她的父親John Bokill II25歲，母親Jane Layston21歲。她和John Jenney至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1498年在她的家鄉享年98歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bokill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy whose medieval forms share a similar range of variants, such as Bouville (Seine-Maritime), any of several places called Biéville (Calvados, Manche) or Biville (Manche, Seine-Maritime), Beuzeville-la-Giffard (Seine-Maritime), Boiville (Orne), and perhaps Boudeville (Seine-Maritime). See also   Boswell   and   Beville  .

variants.name: Boswell
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Beuzeville-la-Giffard in Seine-Maritime, France, named with the ancient Germanic personal name Boso + ville ‘settlement’. The final element has been altered as a result of association with the common placename ending -well ‘spring, stream’. Some instances of Bovill may have the same origin.

variants.name: Beville
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey; of Norman origin): variant of   Bovill  . Derivation from Beuville (Calvados) is also possible, though unproven.

French (also Béville): habitational name from Béville (Moselle) or Béville-le-Comte (Eure-et-Loir), or a variant of Beuville, a habitational name from Beuville (Calvados). The surname Béville is rare in France.

variants.name: Buffalo
meanings: English: apparently an American English nickname from buffalo, perhaps denoting a large, powerfully built man. Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of an unidentified surname, possibly English Bovill . Compare Buffaloe and Buffalow .

Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Lakota and Dakota Sioux tataŋka and Cheyenne hotoa'e, meaning ‘buffalo’ or ‘bull’. See also Bull 5.

History: Majority of the Buffalos trace their origin to the brothers Arthur and William Buffelow, who were recorded in NC in 1750s. Their surname was (later) also spelled Buffalow, Buffaloe, and Buffalo. The word buffalo is even nowadays often misspelled buffelow.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Bokill was born in 1400, in Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, England, her father, John Bokill II, was 25 and her mother, Jane Layston, was 21. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with John Jenney. She died in 1498, in her hometown, at the age of 98.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Bokill est née en 1400, à Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Bokill II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Jane Layston, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec John Jenney. Elle est décédée en 1498, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 98 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Bokill 1400 in Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Bokill II 25 und ihre Mutter Jane Layston 21. Sie hatte mit John Jenney mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1498 im Alter von 98 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Bokill è nata il nel 1400 nel Knodishall with Buxlow, Suffolk, England, sua padre, John Bokill II, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Jane Layston, aveva 21 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con John Jenney. Morì nel 1498, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 98 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Bokill родилась в 1400 г. в местности нодишолл вит букслоу, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bokill II было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Layston, 21.
